title: Algorithmic Game Theory Core Concepts
description: A visual breakdown of the foundational concepts and their relationships in Algorithmic Game Theory.
relationships: Game Theory provides the mathematical models of strategic interaction
Algorithms & Complexity analyzes the efficiency and computability of solutions
Economics contributes concepts like utility and market equilibria
Computer Science provides the computational framework and implementation
Mechanism Design is a key application that combines incentives and algorithms
Price of Anarchy measures the efficiency loss in decentralized systems
Nash Equilibrium is a central solution concept studied for computability
Computational Complexity classifies the hardness of finding game-theoretic solutions
